title_original: Quantinuum Expands Global Footprint to Singapore with the Establishment of a New R&D Centre
summary_original: Quantinuum has announced the establishment of a new research and development and operations centre in Singapore, marking its formal expansion into the country. Supported by the Singapore Economic Development Board, Quantinuum plans to deploy its Helios quantum computer in Singapore later this year to strengthen collaborations across pharmaceuticals, materials science, finance, and other sectors. The centre aligns with Singapore’s National Quantum Strategy and will partner with local startups such as Entropica and Squareroot8 to co-develop quantum communications applications. Dr. Rajeeb Hazra, Quantinuum president and chief executive officer, outlined a focus on use cases, infrastructure, and workforce as essential pillars. The initiative aims to position Singapore as a global quantum technology hub.
